message:
chore: audit batch entries for compress negotiation + beads sync

audit_index.jsonl: 2 new entries — web/compress.rs (FIXED: 406 for
explicitly rejected encodings) and http/compress.rs (FIXED: whitespace
Accept-Encoding normalization).

.beads/issues.jsonl: synced issue tracker state with RusticLynx
web/compress audit comments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1058,7 +1058,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1qbhb","title":"[ALIEN][SCHED] Lyapunov-Guided Scheduler Integration (Cancel Convergence)","description":"## Goal

Integrate the existing Lyapunov governor (potential-function scheduling) into the *real* runtime scheduler so cancellation/drain/quiescence converges quickly and predictably.

Asupersync already treats cancellation as a protocol. The governor provides a principled way to prioritize work that reduces "cancel debt" and outstanding obligations.

## Potential function sketch

Define a non-negative potential V(Σ) over runtime state Σ, e.g.:

V(Σ) = w1 * (# unresolved obligations)
     + w2 * (deadline slack penalties)
     + w3 * (# pending cancel requests)
     + w4 * (IO in-flight debt)
     + ...

A scheduling step should (heuristically) maximize expected drift reduction ΔV subject to correctness constraints.

## Constraints

- Deterministic: decisions must be deterministic given Σ.
- Preserve safety invariants: lane ordering (cancel > timed > ready) should remain correct, or any relaxation must be proven safe and tested.
- No overhead when disabled: default config keeps current scheduler behavior.

## Deliverables

- A scheduler policy seam that can consult the governor.
- Efficient state snapshots (avoid allocations on hot path).
- Deterministic tie-break rules.
- Debug-only "galaxy-brain" ledger trace events for: V breakdown, candidate comparison, chosen action.
- Tests: monotone convergence on synthetic scenarios + no regressions in quiescence/oracles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:09:11.308842108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T10:13:12.266991445Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T10:13:12.266908822Z","close_reason":"done","external_ref":"bd-12rg","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","cancellation","obligation","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qbhb","depends_on_id":"asupersync-kwvuv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qbpc","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][B3] Rebuild Encoder LT/Repair Equation Construction from Tuple Semantics","description":"Rework encoder equation construction to use RFC tuple-derived symbol selection and coefficient behavior instead of identity/custom sampling shortcuts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Source/LT/repair equation construction is tuple-driven, not identity/custom sampling.\n2. Coefficient application rules are explicit and tested.\n3. Encoder outputs align with conformance expectations on vector suite.\n4. Unit + E2E Validation Requirement: implementation includes comprehensive deterministic unit tests and deterministic E2E scripts for this bead scope.\n5. Structured Logging Requirement: all new/changed test flows emit structured logs with seed/config/scenario/outcome and artifact path.\n6. Reproducibility Requirement: every failure path includes a direct repro command and stable fixture/seed reference.","notes":"Implemented tuple-semantics encoder pass on reserved surfaces.

Code changes:
- src/raptorq/systematic.rs:
  - Removed encoder-side random/soliton sampling path from repair symbol generation.
  - repair_symbol_into_with_degree now evaluates params.rfc_repair_equation(esi) directly and accumulates intermediate symbols via gf256_addmul_slice.
  - Degree metadata now reports RFC equation term count (columns.len()).
  - Kept deterministic seed behavior for precode/intermediate construction.
  - Added tests:
    - repair_symbol_matches_rfc_equation_terms
    - emitted_repair_degree_matches_rfc_equation_width
- src/raptorq/tests.rs:
  - Added conformance test repair_symbol_matches_rfc_equation_projection to verify encoder repair bytes match projection of RFC equation terms.

Validation (all via rch exec):
- cargo fmt -- src/raptorq/systematic.rs src/raptorq/tests.rs PASS
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch_cloudyfox cargo check -p asupersync --lib PASS
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch_cloudyfox cargo test -p asupersync --lib raptorq::systematic::tests::repair_symbol_matches_rfc_equation_terms -- --nocapture PASS
- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch_cloudyfox cargo test -p asupersync --lib raptorq::systematic::tests::emitted_repair_degree_matches_rfc_equation_width -- --nocapture PASS (one run remote-fallback local after rsync failure)
- Full workspace gates currently blocked by unrelated pre-existing tree state / clippy backlog:
  - cargo fmt --check reports unrelated formatting in src/runtime/blocking_pool.rs
  - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings fails on broad existing lint backlog across channel/net/trace/raptorq/sync/etc surfaces unrelated to this bead.
  - Some worker runs also hit transient rch infra issues (remote disk-full/no-such-file under /tmp/rch) and local fallback behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleHeron","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-02-14T19:18:58.251541318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:25:38.675344553Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:25:28.834015766Z","close_reason":"Completed","external_ref":"bd-2o5g6","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["encoder","raptorq","rfc6330"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qbpc","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1sics","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:21:27.349861373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qbpc","depends_on_id":"asupersync-26kbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:21:27.295266604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qbpc","depends_on_id":"asupersync-y1xet","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:18:58.279828240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1557,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qbpc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"[2026-02-15 GentleHeron] Verification pass after stale-reopen: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-target-gentleheron-b1 cargo test -p asupersync --lib repair_symbol_matches_rfc_equation -- --nocapture (PASS: repair_symbol_matches_rfc_equation_terms + repair_symbol_matches_rfc_equation_projection); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-target-gentleheron-b1 cargo test -p asupersync --lib emitted_repair_degree_matches_rfc_equation_width -- --nocapture (PASS). Prior rch check from this closure chain: cargo check -p asupersync --lib PASS.","created_at":"2026-02-15T05:25:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qesf","title":"[AA][Cancel] Loser‑drain enforcement across combinators","description":"## Goal
Ensure all race/join/select combinators explicitly cancel and drain losers.

## Why
“Losers are drained” is a non‑negotiable invariant; it prevents hidden obligations and resource leaks.

## Deliverables
- Combinator audit: race/join/select/timeout/fanout.
- Unified loser‑drain API with metrics.
- Tests that assert loser drains under cancellation and failure.

## Acceptance
- Loser drains are verified by deterministic tests.
- No combinator exits early without draining losers.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:06:29.624134375Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:00:09.472495778Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:00:09.472427271Z","close_reason":"Loser-drain audit complete: documented Select/SelectAll loser-drain responsibility, added SelectAllDrain type that returns losers, verified scope::race/race_all/plan::race_branch drain correctly, documented epoch/graceful exceptions, added 6 new tests","external_ref":"bd-zsfd","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qesf","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1syif","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qesf","depends_on_id":"asupersync-28pba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0","title":"[audit] Fresh-eyes random subsystem exploration and bug fixes","description":"Randomly sample unrelated subsystems, trace code paths, identify obvious bugs or edge-case mistakes, and patch with targeted tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseDune","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:54:51.379771910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:01:48.873750314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","exploration"],"comments":[{"id":2442,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"TurquoiseDune","text":"Patched src/messaging/nats.rs read-buffer safety: NatsReadBuffer::consume now clamps cursor to buf.len() (saturating_add(...).min(buf.len())) so oversized consume requests cannot move pos out of bounds and trigger panic via available() slicing. Added regression test test_read_buffer_consume_clamps_when_over_consumed. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync --lib test_read_buffer_consume_clamps_when_over_consumed -- --nocapture (pass); rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (pass); cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings currently failing in unrelated active-lane files src/runtime/reactor/browser.rs and src/supervision.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-07T21:58:23Z"},{"id":2447,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SageHill taking sub-slice: transport channel waiter fairness (FIFO wake order) in src/transport/sink.rs + src/transport/stream.rs with deterministic tests and rch-offloaded validation.","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:45:25Z"},{"id":2448,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"TurquoiseDune","text":"Second audit fix in src/web/session.rs: (1) enforce Secure on Set-Cookie when SameSite=None (set_cookie_header) so emitted cookies are standards-compliant in modern browsers, and (2) fix session-ID entropy helper signature mismatch by changing generate_session_id_with to accept FnMut(&mut [u8]) and adapting callsites/tests (getrandom::fill + copy_from_slice helper test). Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-turquoise-session-lib cargo check -p asupersync --lib passed. Full --all-targets / lib-test runs are currently blocked by unrelated workspace-frontier failures (e.g. src/transport/aggregator.rs E0308, existing warnings in src/lab/runtime.rs under -D warnings).","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:52:16Z"},{"id":2450,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SageHill sub-slice complete: transport waiter fairness in channel sink/stream. Updated pop_next_queued_waiter in src/transport/sink.rs and src/transport/stream.rs to prune stale entries first and then wake FIFO (remove(0)) instead of LIFO pop, preventing starvation of older waiters under contention. Added deterministic regressions test_channel_sink_wakes_oldest_recv_waiter_first and test_channel_stream_wakes_oldest_send_waiter_first; updated stale-waiter test comments to reflect prune-on-pop behavior. Validation via rch: (1) timeout 420 rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => pass, (2) timeout 420 rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings => remote command exit=0 (rch then hung during artifact retrieval), (3) rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync skips_stale_ -- --nocapture exposed unrelated pre-existing failures in src/transport/aggregator.rs E0308 at lines 1684 and 1706 and unrelated warning in src/lab/runtime.rs:1653.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:16:12Z"},{"id":2466,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"VioletLotus","text":"Working sub-slice in src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs: thread an explicit time getter through stagger timing, per-connection timeout, and overall timeout so resolver/lab paths can use deterministic time. Reserved src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs via Agent Mail; Agent Mail send_message is currently failing server-side with a transient DB error, so I’m recording status here. Validation is running via rch now.","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:01:48Z"}]}

Validation is running via rch now.","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:01:48Z"},{"id":2551,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RusticLynx: completed src/web/compress.rs audit fix. Patched negotiation to consider only actually servable encodings, return 406 when Accept-Encoding rejects every available representation, and added regressions for explicit rejection plus compression-disabled identity fallback. Validation: rch cargo test web::compress::tests --lib passed (13 tests); rch cargo check --all-targets passed; rustfmt check on src/web/compress.rs passed. Remaining failures are unrelated repo drift: cargo fmt --check still fails in src/http/h1/http_client.rs and tests/adversarial_lab_vs_live_stress_test.rs / tests/websocket_acceptor_trailing_bytes.rs; cargo clippy --all-targets is still blocked by existing clippy::struct_field_names in src/messaging/class.rs:109.","created_at":"2026-03-18T22:56:46Z"},{"id":2550,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RusticLynx: auditing src/web/compress.rs. Found and patched a protocol mismatch: when Accept-Encoding explicitly rejects every servable representation, web::compress::CompressionMiddleware was falling back to identity instead of 406. Added focused regressions and validating with rch-backed cargo test/check/clippy now.","created_at":"2026-03-18T22:49:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.1","title":"[audit][transport] Prevent PathSet ID reuse when mixing register() and create_path()","description":"src/transport/aggregator.rs currently leaves PathSet::next_id unchanged when callers manually register() a TransportPath. A subsequent create_path() can therefore reuse an existing PathId (for example after registering PathId(0) into a fresh PathSet) and silently overwrite the earlier entry in the path map. Fix by advancing the monotonic ID cursor on manual registration and add a regression test covering mixed register/create usage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SwiftDune","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:54:20.802661784Z","created_by":"SwiftDune","updated_at":"2026-03-08T19:33:25.096978850Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T19:33:25.096944707Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in-tree: src/transport/aggregator.rs::PathSet::register() now advances next_id to at least registered_id+1 via compare_exchange loop, and src/transport/aggregator.rs includes regression test test_path_set_register_advances_next_id covering mixed register/create usage. Closing as stale/currently-fixed after source audit; targeted rch validation is being refreshed separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:54:20.802661784Z","created_by":"SwiftDune","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.10","title":"[audit][runtime] Validate already-present try_join mutability fix in task_handle/fixtures","description":"The current working tree already contains a local mutability fix for the latest try_join frontier: TaskHandle::try_join now takes &mut self in src/runtime/task_handle.rs, its direct tests now bind mutable handles, and SharedLabHandle::try_join_probe in src/plan/fixtures.rs now temporarily takes a mutable handle before calling try_join(). Validate that this already-present fix clears the intended lib-level compile/lint surface via rch-backed targeted tests plus lib check/clippy, and only edit further if validation exposes a real remaining defect.","notes":"Validated the already-present local mutability fix for the try_join frontier without adding further code edits. Current working tree state already contains: (1) src/runtime/task_handle.rs changing TaskHandle::try_join to take &mut self and updating direct tests to use mutable handles; (2) src/plan/fixtures.rs changing SharedLabHandle::try_join_probe to take a mutable handle before calling try_join(). Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync --lib try_join_ -- --nocapture => PASS (6 tests passed: runtime::task_handle try_join_* plus related remote_handle_try_join_* coverage); rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync --lib dynamic_lab_join_collects_all_leaves -- --nocapture => PASS; earlier current-tree lib gates also passed via rch: cargo check -p asupersync --lib -q => PASS, cargo clippy -p asupersync --lib --message-format short -- -D warnings => PASS. No additional code change was required from this bead beyond validating the already-present fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageRaven","created_at":"2026-03-08T04:32:15.438236704Z","created_by":"SageRaven","updated_at":"2026-03-08T04:38:14.430133153Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T04:38:14.430028759Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","runtime","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.10","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T04:32:15.438236704Z","created_by":"SageRaven","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.100","title":"[audit][signal] Preserve one recv() per recorded signal delivery","description":"src/signal/signal.rs tracks monotone delivery counts, but Signal::recv() currently sets seen_deliveries = current and returns a single Some(()) when current > seen_deliveries. If multiple deliveries are recorded before the next recv(), intermediate notifications are collapsed and lost despite the module docs saying no delivered signal notification is lost. Make recv consume exactly one pending delivery per await while preserving cancel safety, add focused regression coverage for multiple injected deliveries, and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","notes":"Implementation verified in src/signal/signal.rs: Signal::recv now consumes exactly one pending delivery per await (seen_deliveries = seen_deliveries.saturating_add(1)), and regression signal_recv_preserves_multiple_recorded_deliveries is present. Coordination evidence from CrimsonCompass confirms rch check/clippy pass and reservation released; ownership bookkeeping retained here to close bead cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"MaroonBridge","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:17.501266861Z","created_by":"CrimsonCompass","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:53:28.848854803Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T18:53:28.795416606Z","close_reason":"Implemented in tree with regression; closeout bookkeeping completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.100","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:17.501266861Z","created_by":"CrimsonCompass","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
